dataset: CAMEO Data Manager - EPCRA Tier II Synthetic Dataset

pi_items.source: Data files central_vt_facilities.xml, ehs_audit_data.xml show 'Ammonia, Anhydrous' with CAS 7664-41-7. Web search confirms 7664-41-7 is the CAS number for Ammonia anhydrous (NH3)
Verified via web search (https://www.sigmaaldrich.com, https://echa.europa.eu, https://www.fishersci.com). Confirmed in data files as CAS number for Ammonia, Anhydrous

pi_items.verified_value: Richmond Processing Plant OR Essex Wire and Cable
pi_items.source: Search of all data files shows Green Valley Water Facility stores Chlorine (7782-50-5) and Fluorosilic Acid (16961-83-4), NOT Ammonia. Actual facilities with Ammonia (7664-41-7): Richmond Processing Plant (central_vt_facilities.xml) and Essex Wire and Cable (ehs_audit_data.xml)

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic EPCRA Tier II XML dataset in /data/*.xml files
pi_items.source: Data files follow EPCRA Tier II XML schema (https://cameo.noaa.gov/epcra_tier2/data_standard/v1). Facilities are fictional with Vermont locations and realistic contact information. Report year varies (2019-2024)

pi_items.source: Complete scan of data files using grep and Python XML parsing for 'Ammonia' and CAS '7664-41-7'

privileged_info_summary: The task requires searching for facilities storing Ammonia (Anhydrous, CAS 7664-41-7) in a CAMEO Data Manager database. The CAS number 7664-41-7 is correct for Ammonia anhydrous. However, the metadata expected facilities are significantly incorrect: Green Valley Water Facility actually stores Chlorine and Fluorosilic Acid (not Ammonia), while Cold Storage Logistics and Agri-Coop West do not exist in the database. The actual facilities storing Ammonia in the database are Richmond Processing Plant and Essex Wire and Cable (both from EPCRA Tier II synthetic data). The chemical is properly called 'Ammonia, Anhydrous' in the data, though 'Ammonia' alone is not incorrect. The excluded facilities (Downtown Office Complex, Riverside Apartments) do not exist in the database.
